articles.title: A flexibility expert’s simple three-minute trick that can improve mobility

articles.title: Energy shock to accelerate transition: EU-ASEAN Business Council
articles.description: Chris Humphrey of EU-ASEANBusiness Council says businesses are withholding investments and waiting for more clarity amid geopolitical tensions. He added that ASEAN firms believe oil shocks from the ongoing Middle East tensions could speed up the shift to green energy.

articles.title: Philippine food security at risk amid fertilizer price spike: ASEAN-Business Advisory Council
articles.description: James Amparo, Executive Advisor for Food Security and Agriculture at the ASEAN-Business Advisory Council, discusses food security and the challenges facing agriculture in the Philippines, particularly due to fertilizer shortages and the looming El Niño drought.

articles.title: She thought she just had mild food poisoning – but days later she was dead
articles.description: Claire McCormack, 47, thought she had a stomach bug but within hours was placed in an induced coma as sepsis caused multi-organ failure. Her family are now raising awareness of the warning signs

articles.title: Dr Karl: The surprising four-minute supermarket act that could reduce cancer risk by almost HALF
articles.description: Australians who dread the gym may be relieved to hear that Dr Karl Kruszelnicki says major health benefits could come from something far simpler.

articles.title: UAE currency swap line might delay the collapse of the market's 'pyramid of leverage': CIO
articles.description: Mark Tinker, CIO of Toscafund Hong Kong, discusses the potential liquidity problem due to the Iran war and the sustainability of the current level of global debt.

articles.title: B.C. gov't to allow midwives to prescribe abortion pill and other drugs
articles.description: Once the changes come into effect, midwives will be able to independently prescribe Mifegymiso, also known as RU 486, which can treat an early miscarriage in addition to providing a medical abortion during the first trimester of pregnancy.

articles.title: Cash prescription program associated with fewer infant maltreatment investigations
articles.description: A new peer-reviewed study published in JAMA Pediatrics provides rigorous, population-level evidence that Michigan State University's Rx Kids program, the nation's first community-wide prenatal and infant cash prescription program, is associated with substantial reductions in child maltreatment investigations among infants.

articles.title: Speech and language therapists concerned about screen time for young children
articles.description: Too many parents are still unaware of the negative effects that screens can have on the development of their young children, laments the Quebec Association of Speech and Language Therapists and Audiologists.

articles.title: Heart disease risk prediction tool proves effective across global populations
articles.description: A tool developed by the American Heart Association (AHA), proven to accurately predict heart disease risk for Americans, can be applied to the global population, a new study led by NYU Langone Health shows.

articles.title: Methylmercury may harm metabolism beyond its known neurological effects
articles.description: Methylmercury can accumulate in water environments and enter the food chain. Human exposure is of particular concern in communities affected by contaminated rivers, industrial pollution, artisanal gold mining and environmental disasters.

articles.title: Blood test may improve early tuberculosis detection among household contacts
articles.description: Household contacts of people with tuberculosis (TB) have a high risk of getting TB themselves, at around 2 percent. It is currently difficult to detect TB in its early stages, or predict who will go on to have TB, and therefore preventive treatment is not widely used.

articles.title: Conversation therapy helps dementia patients reconnect with loved ones
articles.description: When words fade due to dementia, talking to the people you love becomes more difficult. Fortunately, support is available.

articles.title: Anthony Fauci blames Americans for not hitting his COVID vaccine target while ignoring other countries
articles.description: Fauci says anti-vax culture kept the US from reaching 72% vaccination, but highly vaccinated countries still saw record COVID hospitalizations.

articles.title: Supporting at-risk young mums and their babies
